prompt: Build v3 of the Watchtower board: add IDEA-STALENESS to the Lanes view (show which concepts have gone cold). The board is experiments/watchtower-board/ (server.mjs + parse-concepts.mjs). SOURCE OF 'last touched' = git history of backlog/concepts.md — git owns change-history; do NOT invent dates or add manual date fields. STEPS: (1) parse-concepts.mjs: make parseConcepts(md) also record each item's 1-based source line number in concepts.md → item = {concept,status,pri,line}. Keep the existing direct-run self-test ('node parse-concepts.mjs' prints '<N> lanes, <M> items'). (2) Add to parse-concepts.mjs an EXPORTED PURE function attachStaleness(lanes, lineDateMap, nowMs, staleDays): lineDateMap is {lineNumber: epochMs}; for each item set lastTouched (lineDateMap[item.line] or nowMs if absent), ageDays (Math.floor((nowMs-lastTouched)/86400000)), stale (ageDays >= staleDays); also set per-lane lastTouched=max(item lastTouched), ageDays, stale; return enriched lanes. MUST be pure (no git, no IO) so it is unit-testable. (3) server.mjs ONLY: add runtime helper gitLineDates() that runs `git blame --line-porcelain backlog/concepts.md` via execFile('git', ...) with cwd = repo root (two levels up from the board dir), returns {lineNumber: epochMs} from each line's committer-time; lines 'not committed yet' → omit. At GET /api/concepts: parse → call gitLineDates() → attachStaleness(lanes, map, Date.now(), 14) → return {lanes, counts}. If git fails, fall back to an empty map (everything fresh) — NEVER crash the endpoint. (4) Lanes view UI (ADDITIVE, do NOT touch Floor): on each concept card show a small age badge ('3d', or 'new' if ageDays===0); add a CSS class 'stale' (opacity ~.5 + muted border) on cards where item.stale; show a stale hint in the lane header when the lane is stale. Reuse existing CSS vars/classes. (5) CONSTRAINTS: additive only; Floor view + /api/state untouched; no external deps; data from real sources (git + file), never invented. (6) TEST WITHOUT GIT — you may NOT have git permission, so your self-test must NOT call git: test attachStaleness with an INLINE MOCK lineDateMap (some old lines, some recent) and print 'staleness ok: <X> stale of <M>'. The live git-blame runs only at server runtime (Marshall verifies that separately). (7) Write proof to experiments/watchtower-engine/proof/board-v3-staleness.md (max 6 lines: what changed, the pure function, mock-test result, confirmation Floor untouched + endpoint never crashes on git failure). Then complete run-next-workflow bookkeeping.
